layout_description:
Option: OPT-1
Slide description: FULL-WIDTH TABLE LAYOUT with generous padding and vertical rhythm. The slide uses a single-dominant orientation with the title at the top and one primary content container below. The table spans the full usable width of the slide (approximately 93% of slide width) and fills the vertical space between the title and the bottom margin. The layout emphasizes clarity and readability through consistent column widths, alternating row backgrounds, and dashed separator lines between rows. All content is distributed evenly across the vertical dimension using flexGrow to prevent top-stacking. The design follows the Flat Geometry Table aesthetic (Option C) with no filled header bars, using accent tabs and solid underlines instead.

Blueprint:
  Orientation: top-to-bottom
  Regions: 2
  Allocation: [0.12, 0.88]

Containers:
  C0 (FULL-WIDTH DATA TABLE): Primary content container holding a 4-column regulatory tax table. Spatial relationship: positioned directly below the title with a visible top margin (approximately 3-5% of slide height), centered horizontally on the slide, spanning full usable width. Proportional weight: 88% of vertical space, 93% of horizontal space. Content summary: Table with 4 columns (Tax Type, Rate, Applicable Activity, Legal Basis) and 4 data rows (Income Tax, Capital Gains, Excise Duty, Withholding Tax). Column 1 contains category labels, Column 2 contains rate values, Columns 3-4 contain descriptive text of varying lengths. Design intent: Flat geometry aesthetic with no filled header bars. Column headers rendered as bold text in brand primary color, left-aligned within each column. Directly beneath each header label, place a small accent tab (brand primary fill, very thin height, narrow width approximately 20% of column width, top corners slightly rounded, bottom edge square, left-aligned with header text). Immediately below and touching the accent tab, render a thin solid horizontal line in brand primary color spanning the full column width (with small white gutters between columns, not a single full-width line). Data rows distributed evenly across remaining vertical space using flexGrow. Row 1 (Income Tax): light grey fill background spanning all 4 columns, bold text in first column (Tax Type), regular text in remaining columns. Row 2 (Capital Gains): white background, bold text in first column, regular text in remaining columns. Row 3 (Excise Duty): light grey fill background, bold text in first column, regular text in remaining columns. Row 4 (Withholding Tax): white background, bold text in first column, regular text in remaining columns. Alternating row backgrounds (light grey alt and white) create visual grouping. Between each data row, render a thin dashed grey horizontal separator line spanning the full table width. No separator after the last row. All text uses body font, brand text color for body content, brand primary for headers. Column widths: Column 1 (Tax Type) sized to fit longest label without wrapping (approximately 20% of table width), Column 2 (Rate) compact (approximately 12% of table width), Columns 3-4 (Applicable Activity and Legal Basis) share remaining space equally (approximately 34% each). All row sections within the table must use identical flexGrow values to maintain horizontal alignment across columns. Text within cells: left-aligned with small left inset padding, vertically centered within row height. No cell borders except the dashed row separators. Font size: medium body text, reduced slightly if necessary to fit the longest text in Column 3 without wrapping, but all body text across the table must use the same font size (C6 rule). The table structure is built entirely from shapes and text boxes using flex layout, not native PowerPoint table elements.
  SC-BOTTOM (DECORATIVE BACKGROUND LAYER): Inactive. The table content fills the slide effectively and does not require additional visual grouping or background fill. The alternating row backgrounds within the table provide sufficient visual structure.
  SC-TOP (OVERLAY CONNECTOR LAYER): Inactive. No cross-container connectors or overlay elements are needed for this single-table layout.
